info.name: Utility Billing System
info.description:
Role-based Postman collection for the Utility Billing System backend.

Folders are organised by the persona who uses them:
- 🔐 Authentication (All Users): register, email OTP verification, login per role, identity, logout.
- 👑 Admin: staff users, customer verification/management, meters, tariffs, billing configuration.
- 🔧 Operator: capture meter readings (and view meters for context).
- 💰 Finance: bills (approve/cancel/generate/overdue) and payments, plus notification oversight.
- 👤 Customer: self-service profile completion and viewing own meters, bills, payments, notifications.
- 🔁 End-to-End Happy Path: an automated sequence for a quick demo.

Import together with the local environment file. Login requests save the JWT automatically into {{accessToken}} so protected requests run without pasting a token. Run the matching login for the role before calling a folder's requests.

item.name: 🔐 Authentication (All Users)
item.description: Shared by every persona. A public visitor registers and verifies email; staff and customers log in here. Each login stores {{accessToken}} for the rest of the collection.

exec: // Email must be verified via OTP before login succeeds.

request.description: Creates a public customer user only (always ROLE_CUSTOMER). An OTP is emailed for verification.
Set {{otpCode}} to the 6-digit code sent to the registered email, then send. Login is blocked until the email is verified.
Works only after the registered email has been verified via OTP.
Revokes the current {{accessToken}}. Subsequent calls with it return 401.

item.name: 👑 Admin (System Administration)
item.description: Run 'Login — Admin' first. Admin configures the system: staff users, customer verification/management, meters, tariffs and billing configuration.

item.name: Staff Users
item.description: Create and manage Operator/Finance/Admin accounts. Only an Admin can reach these.

request.description: Set {{staffRole}} to ROLE_OPERATOR, ROLE_FINANCE or ROLE_ADMIN. Staff accounts are created ACTIVE. ROLE_CUSTOMER is rejected here.
Blocked by the backend if it would disable the last active admin.

item.name: Customers (Verify & Manage)
item.description: Verify self-registered customers (activate), or create/link customer profiles directly.

request.description: Step that turns a self-submitted (INACTIVE) profile into an ACTIVE billable customer.
Creates a customer not yet linked to a login account (userId null).

item.name: List Customer Users (Find userId for Linking)

exec: pm.test('Captured a customer userId for linking', function () { pm.expect(rows.length).to.be.above(0); });

query.description: true = only users without a customer profile yet

request.description: Lists ROLE_CUSTOMER users so an Admin can find a userId to link. Saves the first match into {{linkUserId}}. Set unlinkedOnly=false to list all customer users.
Direct transform of a registered user into a customer. Run 'List Customer Users' first to populate {{linkUserId}}. The backend rejects non-customer roles and users already linked to a profile.
Hard-deletes if the customer has no meters/bills/payments; otherwise soft-deletes (sets INACTIVE) to preserve financial history.

item.name: Get Customer Meters By Customer Id
Meters
item.description: Only an Admin assigns and manages meters. A meter must belong to an ACTIVE customer.

item.name: Tariffs
item.description: Versioned consumption tariffs (FLAT or TIERED). New versions apply to future billing only.

request.description: For a TIERED tariff set tariffType=TIERED and omit ratePerUnit, then add tiers (must start at 0 and be contiguous).

item.name: Billing Configuration
item.description: Fixed service charges, taxes (VAT) and late-payment penalties — all versioned and time-effective.

item.name: 🔧 Operator (Meter Readings)
item.description: Run 'Login — Operator' first. Operators capture readings; a valid reading auto-generates a PENDING_APPROVAL bill. They may also view meters for context.

request.description: currentReading must exceed the previous reading; one reading per meter per month/year; meter and customer must be ACTIVE.

item.name: 💰 Finance (Billing & Payments)
item.description: Run 'Login — Finance' first (Admin can also do all of these). Finance approves bills, records payments, processes overdue bills, and oversees notifications.

exec: const pending = rows.find(function (r) { return r.status === 'PENDING_APPROVAL'; }) || rows[0];
if (pending) { pm.environment.set('billId', pending.id); pm.environment.set('billReference', pending.billReference); pm.environment.set('outstandingBalance', String(pending.outstandingBalance)); }

request.description: Idempotent: returns the existing bill if one already exists for the reading.
Allowed only when nothing has been paid on the bill.
Applies one-time penalties to bills past their due date plus grace period.
Bill must be APPROVED/PARTIALLY_PAID/OVERDUE. Amount > 0 and cannot exceed the outstanding balance. Full payment marks the bill PAID.

exec: if (json.data && json.data.length > 0) { pm.environment.set('notificationId', json.data[0].id); if (json.data[0].customerId) { pm.environment.set('customerId', json.data[0].customerId); } }

item.name: 👤 Customer (Self Service)
item.description: Run 'Login — Seeded Customer' or 'Login — My Registered Customer' first. A customer completes their profile, then (after admin activation + meter assignment) views their own data.

item.name: Complete My Profile (Submit National ID + Address)

exec: pm.test('Profile created and pending verification', function () { pm.expect([200, 201]).to.include(pm.response.code); });

request.description: Creates the caller's customer profile (status INACTIVE) linked to their user. An Admin must then activate it before billing can start.

item.name: 🔁 End-to-End Happy Path (Automated)
item.description: A quick demo run using the Admin account (Admin can do every step). Run top to bottom: it logs in, picks an active meter, captures a reading, finds and approves the generated bill, then records a partial and the remaining payment.

exec: pm.test('At least one active meter exists', function () { pm.expect(json.data.length).to.be.above(0); });
const d = new Date(); d.setDate(5); d.setMonth(d.getMonth() - offset);
pm.test('An unused billing month is available', function () { pm.expect(chosen).to.not.equal(null); });
let bill = rows.find(function (row) { return String(row.meterReadingId) === String(pm.environment.get('readingId')); });
if (!bill) { bill = rows.find(function (row) { return row.status === 'PENDING_APPROVAL'; }) || rows[0]; }
pm.test('A bill was found for the flow', function () { pm.expect(bill).to.exist; });
